title: RSVP ERP dataset for authentication from Zhang et al 2025
description: A longitudinal EEG dataset comprising event-related potentials (ERPs) from 15 healthy participants performing a rapid serial visual presentation (RSVP) face authentication task. The dataset includes 4 sessions per subject collected over 200 days, with 57-channel EEG recordings at 1000 Hz sampling rate. This derivative dataset is designed for developing and benchmarking brain-computer interface (BCI) classifiers for identity authentication and target detection applications using P300-based paradigms.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Neuracle Neusen system with 57 channels arranged in a standard 10-20 montage (reference: CPz, ground: AFz). Participants performed an RSVP face authentication task involving self-face versus AI-generated face discrimination across 4 blocks per session, with 200 images per block presented at 100 ms stimulus onset asynchrony. Sampling rate was 1000 Hz with 50 Hz line frequency filtering.
